req.title: Approve leave request — variant 3
req.statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) a happy-path approval by the assigned manager, (2) rejection of the action when the acting user is not the assignee (403), (3) rejection when the request is already actioned (409), (4) auto-escalation boundary at exactly 5 business days, and (5) webhook emission on approval. Tests are designed using EP for the happy path, NEG for the 403/409 error paths, BVA for the 5-business-day escalation boundary, and STC for state transitions (pending→approved, pending→escalated). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up their own leave request.

tests.name: test_manager_approves_assigned_request_succeeds
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:manager
A leave request exists in state=pending and is assigned to this manager
Webhook receiver endpoint is reachable and recorded
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
Query GET /leave-requests/{id} to read persisted state
Query GET /audit-log?entity_id={id} to verify audit entry
Query webhook capture endpoint for emitted events
expected: Employee notification record exists (notifications API or email stub)
Audit log contains one entry with action=approve and actor=manager
Webhook capture shows event type=leave_request.approved for the request id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_approves_assigned_request_succeeds(
    manager_session,          # <fixture>: returns authed requests.Session for manager
    pending_leave_request,    # <fixture>: creates pending request assigned to manager, yields id
    webhook_capture,          # <fixture>: starts mock receiver, yields helper
    api_base_url,             # <fixture>: base URL string
):
    req_id = pending_leave_request
    url = f"{api_base_url}/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve"

    resp = manager_session.post(url)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "approved"

    detail = manager_session.get(f"{api_base_url}/leave-requests/{req_id}").json()
    assert detail["state"] == "approved"

    notifications = manager_session.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/notifications", params={"entity_id": req_id}
    ).json()
    assert any(n["recipient_role"] == "employee" for n in notifications)

    audit = manager_session.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/audit-log", params={"entity_id": req_id}
    ).json()
    assert any(e["action"] == "approve" for e in audit)

    events = webhook_capture.received_events()
    assert any(
        e["event_type"] == "leave_request.approved" and e["payload"]["id"] == req_id
        for e in events
    )

tests.name: test_non_assignee_manager_approve_returns_403
preconditions: A leave request exists in state=pending and is assigned to manager_a (different manager)
manager_b is NOT the assignee of the request
expected: Response body contains error code indicating not-assignee
No audit entry created for this attempt
trace: R-WEB-064: error pathway — 403 if manager not the assignee
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_assignee_manager_approve_returns_403(
    manager_b_session,                # <fixture>: authed session for manager_b
    pending_request_for_manager_a,    # <fixture>: pending request assigned to manager_a, yields id
    webhook_capture,
    api_base_url,
):
    req_id = pending_request_for_manager_a
    url = f"{api_base_url}/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve"

    resp = manager_b_session.post(url)
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    err = resp.json()
    assert "not_assignee" in err.get("error_code", "").lower() or \
           "forbidden" in err.get("message", "").lower()

    detail = manager_b_session.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/leave-requests/{req_id}"
    ).json()
    assert detail["state"] == "pending"

    events = webhook_capture.received_events()
    assert not any(
        e["payload"].get("id") == req_id for e in events
    )

tests.name: test_approve_already_actioned_request_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:manager
A leave request exists in state=approved (already actioned) and is assigned to this manager
actions: POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve with manager's auth token (second approval attempt)
expected: Response body contains error indicating request already actioned
Leave request state remains approved (no mutation)
trace: R-WEB-064: error pathway — 409 if already actioned
R-WEB-064: state must not regress or duplicate
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_approve_already_actioned_request_returns_409(
    manager_session,
    approved_leave_request,    # <fixture>: creates request in state=approved, yields id
    webhook_capture,
    api_base_url,
):
    req_id = approved_leave_request
    url = f"{api_base_url}/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve"

    resp = manager_session.post(url)
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    err = resp.json()
    assert "already" in err.get("message", "").lower() or \
           err.get("error_code", "") in ("already_actioned", "conflict")

    detail = manager_session.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/leave-requests/{req_id}"
    ).json()
    assert detail["state"] == "approved"

    audit = manager_session.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/audit-log", params={"entity_id": req_id}
    ).json()
    approve_entries = [e for e in audit if e["action"] == "approve"]
    assert len(approve_entries) == 1   # exactly one, no duplicate

    events = webhook_capture.received_events()
    approve_events = [
        e for e in events
        if e["event_type"] == "leave_request.approved"
        and e["payload"].get("id") == req_id
    ]
    assert len(approve_events) == 1

tests.name: test_unactioned_request_escalates_at_exactly_5_business_days
preconditions: A leave request exists in state=pending assigned to manager
System clock or created_at timestamp is manipulated so the request age is exactly 5 business days
Auto-escalation job/cron is triggerable via test API or time-travel fixture
actions: Advance simulated time to exactly T+5 business days from request creation
Trigger auto-escalation check (POST /internal/escalate-overdue or advance scheduler)
Verify assignee changed to hr-admin role
expected: Leave request state transitions to escalated (or assignee changes to hr-admin)
Audit log contains entry with action=escalate at the 5-business-day mark
Original manager is no longer the assignee
HR-admin receives notification of escalated request
trace: R-WEB-064: unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin
R-WEB-064: within 5 business days — boundary at exactly day 5
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unactioned_request_escalates_at_exactly_5_business_days(
    admin_session,              # <fixture>: internal/admin authed session
    pending_leave_request,      # <fixture>: pending request assigned to manager, yields id
    time_travel,                # <fixture>: context-manager to advance system/app clock
    webhook_capture,
    api_base_url,
):
    req_id = pending_leave_request

    with time_travel(business_days=5):
        escalate_resp = admin_session.post(
            f"{api_base_url}/internal/escalate-overdue"
        )
    assert escalate_resp.status_code in (200, 204)

    detail = admin_session.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/leave-requests/{req_id}"
    ).json()
    assert detail["state"] in ("escalated", "pending")  # state or assignee signals escalation
    assert detail.get("assignee_role") == "hr-admin" or \
           detail.get("state") == "escalated"

    audit = admin_session.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/audit-log", params={"entity_id": req_id}
    ).json()
    assert any(e["action"] == "escalate" for e in audit)

    events = webhook_capture.received_events()
    assert any(
        e["event_type"] == "leave_request.escalated"
        and e["payload"].get("id") == req_id
        for e in events
    )

tests.name: test_approval_transitions_state_from_pending_to_approved
preconditions: Leave request is confirmed in state=pending before action
Manager session is authenticated and is the assignee
trace: R-WEB-064: request state=approved after manager approval
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_approval_transitions_state_from_pending_to_approved(
    manager_session,
    pending_leave_request,
    api_base_url,
):
    req_id = pending_leave_request
    base = api_base_url

    pre = manager_session.get(f"{base}/leave-requests/{req_id}").json()
    assert pre["state"] == "pending", f"Expected pending, got {pre['state']}"

    approve_resp = manager_session.post(
        f"{base}/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve"
    )
    assert approve_resp.status_code == 200

    post = manager_session.get(f"{base}/leave-requests/{req_id}").json()
    assert post["state"] == "approved", f"Expected approved, got {post['state']}"
    assert post["state"] != pre["state"], "State must have changed"
